content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 29, 1988

Herb Steele
424 Adams Street
Coalinga, CA 93210

Re: Your Request for Information
Our File No. G-88-371

Dear Mr. Steele:

Your letter to Chairman John Larson has been referred to me
for a response.

Your letter concerns emergency, interim regulations adopted
by the Commission on September 8, 1988. These regulations are
scheduled for adoption by the Commission as permanent
regulations on November 9, 1988. Enclosed is a copy of the
notice and text of the proposed permanent regulations. We will
consider your letter to be a comment submitted for the record
in connection with adoption of the permanent regulations. Any
additional written comments must be submitted to the Commission
by November 9, 1988.

In your letter you also requested information about the
Commission's accomplishments. Enclosed is a copy of our most
recent annual report.

Sincerely,

[illegible]

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:plh

Enclosures

Dear Sir:

I am writing in regard to your ad on 9/8/88 which was your over Rule of Proposition 73.

I talked with one of your Attorneys this morning. I will tell you, as I told her exactly how I feel. I believe her name was KATHY.

You know, Sir, to go to the polls and vote anymore to express your feelings is a damned waste of time. How can you, in good conscience, go someplace you have done. Doesn't the will of the people have any bearing anymore. Unless I'm sadly mistaken, you, the Commission and that stack of Lawyers are paid to watch out for the majority of the people, not that bunch of misfits we have at the Capitol.

In an article I read, it said that

"The commission CAVED IN on
Prop. 73. ITS damned obvious
who you caved in to, certainly
NOT to the people you are
paid to be a watchdog
This REEKS of the same odor
as the death penalty PASSED not
once but TWICE, by the voters.We
gave Deukmajian his own
Supreme Court and they have
not followed the will of the
people yet.
By the time all of you lawyers
get through picking this around
everyones pockets will be full
AND you CAN go on to bigger
and better pillage.
I intend to make everyone I
meet AWARE of just what you
and your commission are capable of
doing. YOU AREN'T WATCHDOGS.
I CAN ASSOCIATE you with it
dogs but it is no lower a part
of their body. IT MAKES LIARS die."

Jimmy Carter supposedly said "One smart thing." There are too many lawyers in the United States" It didn't take a genius to figure that out, did it?

Sometime, at your convenience, please write and tell me some of the things "you waterlogged" have done for us stupid bastards paying your salary. I know you won't respond to this and I'll make people aware of that, also!

Herb Steele

sections.question_synthetic: The document contains an incoming complaint/comment letter regarding the FPPC's emergency regulations adopted on September 8, 1988, specifically criticizing the Commission's actions regarding Proposition 43.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC response acknowledges receipt of the letter, informs the sender that it will be treated as a comment on the proposed permanent regulations (due November 9, 1988), and provides the proposed regulations and annual report for reference.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both an outgoing FPPC response letter and the incoming complaint letter that prompted it. No formal question/conclusion sections present. The FPPC response is brief and procedural rather than substantive legal advice. The incoming letter is highly critical in tone but does not pose a specific legal question requiring FPPC guidance.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC response acknowledges receipt of the letter, informs the sender that it will be treated as a comment on the proposed permanent regulations (due November 9, 1988), and provides the proposed regulations and annual report for reference.
embedding.first_500_words: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** September 29, 1988 Herb Steele 424 Adams Street Coalinga, CA 93210 Re: Your Request for Information Our File No. G-88-371 Dear Mr. Steele: Your letter to Chairman John Larson has been referred to me for a response. Your letter concerns emergency, interim regulations adopted by the Commission on September 8, 1988. These regulations are scheduled for adoption by the Commission as permanent regulations on November 9, 1988. Enclosed is a copy of the notice and text of the proposed permanent regulations. We will consider your letter to be a comment submitted for the record in connection with adoption of the permanent regulations. Any additional written comments must be submitted to the Commission by November 9, 1988. In your letter you also requested information about the Commission's accomplishments. Enclosed is a copy of our most recent annual report. Sincerely, Kathryn E. Donovan Counsel, Legal Division KED:plh Enclosures 9/12/88 Dear Sir: I am writing in regard to your action on 9-8-88 which was your overrule of Proposition 43. I talked with one of your attorneys this morning. I will tell you, as I told her exactly how I feel. I believe her name was Kathy. You know, Sir, to go to the polls and vote anymore to express your feelings is a damned waste of time. How can you, in good conscience, do something like you have done. Doesn't the will of the people have any bearing anymore. Unless I'm sadly mistaken, you, the Commission and that stack of lawyers are paid to watch out for the majority of the people, not that bunch of misfits we have at the Capitol. In an article I read, it said that "The commission caved in on Prof. 73." It's damned obvious who you caved in to, certainly not to the people you are paid to be a watch dog. This reeks of the same odor as the death penalty. Passed not once but twice, by the voters. We gave Deukmejian "his own" Supreme Court and they have not followed the will of the people yet. By the time all of you lawyers get through kicking this around everyone's pockets will be full and you can go on to bigger and better pillage. I intend to make everyone I meet aware of just what you and your commission are capable of doing. You aren't watchdogs. I can associate you with dogs but it is no longer a part of their body. It makes hawks die!! Jimmy Carter supposedly said, "One smart thing: 'There are too many lawyers in the United States.'" It didn't take a genius to figure that out, did it? Sometime, at your convenience, please write and tell me some of the things "you watchdogs" have done for us stupid bastards paying your salary. I know you won't respond to this and I'll make people aware of that, also! Herb Steele
embedding.summary: This document consists of an FPPC response letter from Counsel Kathryn E. Donovan to Herb Steele regarding his complaint about emergency regulations adopted on September 8, 1988, followed by the original incoming complaint letter criticizing the Commission's actions and expressing frustration with the regulatory process.
